title: Response Bytes, Content Codings, Character Encodings, and Source Identity · Code Recall
summary: A delayed crawl review reconstructs response bytes, content codings, character encodings, and source identity from authorization, revision, seed, URL key, response identity, robots decision, parse record, extraction provenance, frontier event, report row, failure, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for byte-to-Unicode admission ledger after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Track wire, compressed, decompressed, decoded, and retained sizes and stop before an expansion or memory budget is exceeded.
Validate and apply declared content codings in order while distinguishing unsupported, corrupt, partial, and over-budget representations.
Record transport declarations, byte-order marks, HTML declarations, parser detection, replacement behavior, and the final chosen encoding.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.instruction:
review case — A review site-quality team handles case 2836 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile compressed and expanded bytes, character decisions, non-HTML rejection, and source identity; personal data must remain minimized and redacted; then introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Retain effective URL, response metadata, byte digest, decode decision, parser choice, retrieval time, and fixture or authorization identity.

Inspect the Source Digest Provenance trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Source Digest Provenance.

stimulus.title: Source Digest Provenance case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review site-quality team handles case 2836 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. lines.label: risk
lines.text: A normalized text hash is enough to reproduce the fetched source.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Retain effective URL, response metadata, byte digest, decode decision, parser choice, retrieval time, and fixture or authorization identity. Artifact under review: byte-to-Unicode admission ledger.

options.text: The Source Digest Provenance record says byte-to-Unicode admission ledger looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Source Digest Provenance.

For separate evidence from confidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof. This does not satisfy Source Digest Provenance for byte-to-Unicode admission ledger.
The record names the assumption, applies Source Digest Provenance, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Retain effective URL, response metadata, byte digest, decode decision, parser choice, retrieval time, and fixture or authorization identity.
